summary_text:
Chief Complaint
Blood pressure fluctuations and headache.

History of Present Illness
The patient is a 39-year-old female with a history of quadriplegia due to spinal cord injury. She reports increasing concerns for spasms, lightheadedness, significant constipation, and diaphoresis. She was evaluated in the emergency department where she was febrile and had an elevated white blood cell count of 23,000. She has a supra-pubic catheter and was eventually discharged after clinical improvement of her symptoms. However, she does not think it was a UTI, but rather constipation was the cause of her blood pressure issues. She feels like she has a good bowel regimen, including taking miralax regularly and lactulose for severe constipation. She reports that it was giving her a headache and making her throw up. She does drink a lot of vitamin C and multivitamins and is closely monitored. She does not feel as though she really needs the vaccine. She also gets a flu shot every year. The ER is full of these vaccines. She has seen all the bad things that could happen from covid. I have had healthy people die from covid. So personally, I believe that this vaccine is completely safe. She does not want to get covid. She believes her hospitalization was more likely due to her dysreflexia, from her quadriplegia that typically occurs. She has had healthy people die from covid. She does not feel as though she really needs the vaccine. She also gets a flu shot every year. The ER is full of these vaccines. She has seen all the bad things that could happen from covid. I have had healthy people die from covid. So personally, I believe that this vaccine is completely safe. She does not want to get covid. She also takes a lot of vitamin C. I have had healthy people die from covid. She does not feel as though she really needs the vaccine. She also gets a flu shot every year. The ER is full of these vaccines. She has seen all the bad things that could happen from covid. I have had healthy people die from covid. So personally, I believe that this vaccine is completely safe. She does not want to get covid.

Findings
There were concerns for ongoing urinary tract infection, but she was treated with iv antibiotic therapy. Cultures were obtained and reviewed. So, it looks like you had an infection, right? The white cell count was the only thing that I think they were kind of concerned about because it was a little high. So I wondered if automatic dysreflexia can affect the white count? She did have a lot of vital signs changes, including blood pressure fluctuations. I told them that when I was admitted, and they did x-rays and everything, but they said, "no," as they did n't think that was n't... that was it. Uh, they did n't do a cat scan, they just did an x-ray. I mean, I knew going in that I had n't had a regular bowel movement in a while, even though I drink the miralax every day. I mean, i really do n't think it was a uti. It is, in my humble opinion, that i was just constipated and that was what was causing my blood pressure to be so high. Then when i got home i had a massive poop and i felt much better. But in saying that, you know, inflammation can do that. So, yeah, you could be backed up and inflamed.

Assessment
Acute to chronic constipation and leukocytosis.

Plan
I would recommend just in the next three or four weeks to come back in for some updated fasting labs, and we'll repeat the cbc just to make sure her white blood cells tend downward. Have i ever given you lactulose? It would be a medicine you use in, um, conjunction with miralax. Start lactulose and miralax as appropriate. Schedule follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist. Arrange follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist. I would like to talk to you about covid, if we can. So where are you at when it comes to the vaccine? So what are your concerns about the vaccine? I do not believe that supplements are necessarily going to help stave this off. I would be concerned if she does n't get the vaccine and I would consider her at high risk based on her quadriplegia status. I think there are side effects from all vaccines, so you have to go back and think about it. So there are definitely potential side effects to the vaccine. I'm not convinced that there's alternative stuff or even the treatment we have, like the monoclonal antibodies, that will give help, but it's just my opinion. So that's what's gon na happen with covid.
reference_summary:
CHIEF COMPLAINT

Transition of care visit.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Michelle King is a 39-year-old female who presents for a transition care visit. She was admitted to District Regional Hospital on 06/29/2021 and discharged on 07/02/2021.

The patient has a history quadriplegia from a prior spinal cord injury. She reported an increased concern for spasms, lightheadedness, headaches, significant constipation, and diaphoresis. Due to her uncontrolled blood pressure, headaches, and vomiting, she was evaluated in the emergency department. She was noted to be febrile, had a significantly elevated white blood cell count of 23,000, and labile blood pressure. Cultures were obtained and reviewed, with a urinary tract infection suspected. To note, the patient does require use of a suprapubic catheter. She was provided IV antibiotic therapy and was discharged after clinical improvement of her symptoms.

Today, the patient states she is feeling good and claims that she does not think she had an infection. Instead, she believes she was just constipated and that was the cause of her elevated blood pressure. She states she did not have a bowel movement in the hospital and when she got home, she had a large bowel movement and felt better. She states that she told the medical team in the emergency room that she was constipated, but they did not feel that was the causative agent, although they did complete x-rays and additional tests. The patient reports taking MiraLAX consistently for her constipation and Lactulose as needed, which takes 2 days produce a bowel movement.

The patient has not received the COVID-19 vaccinations because she does not know the side effects. She reports taking vitamin C and a multivitamin and she does not feel the need for the vaccine even though her “respiratory status is limited”. The patient also denies receiving the flu vaccine.

PAST HISTORY

Medical
Quadriplegia.
Spinal cord injury.
Hypertension.
Chronic constipation.
Automatic dysreflexia.

CURRENT MEDICATIONS

MiraLAX as needed by mouth.
Lactulose as needed by mouth.

ASSESSMENT

• Acute to chronic constipation.
• Quadriplegia.

PLAN

Acute to chronic constipation
She believes that her hospitalization was more likely due to her constipation and dysreflexia from her quadriplegia. She had a lot of vital sign changes including blood pressure fluctuations. She feels like she has got a good bowel regimen. She does take MiraLAX consistently, and Lactulose for severe constipation. She feels like this was less likely a true urinary tract infection. However, we did have a good discussion regarding her leukocytosis. I would recommend in the next 3 or 4 weeks to come back in for some updated fasting labs and we will repeat the CBC and ensure that trends downwards.

COVID vaccine status
Again, she is hesitant. We had a good discussion. I recommend that the only real, true preventative mechanism for COVID is the vaccine. I do not believe that supplements are necessarily going to help stave this off. I would be concerned that she does not get vaccinated, I would consider her at high risk based on her quadriplegia status. She does not want to get COVID. However, she needs to make these decisions. She is somewhat homeopathic in some of her viewpoints.

INSTRUCTIONS

Labs pending at the time of discharge have been reviewed. Follow-up labs and appointments have been ordered.

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Findings
There were concerns for ongoing urinary tract infection, but she was treated with iv antibiotic therapy. Cultures were obtained and reviewed. So, it looks like you had an infection, right? The white cell count was the only thing that I think they were kind of concerned about because it was a little high. So I wondered if automatic dysreflexia can affect the white count? She did have a lot of vital signs changes, including blood pressure fluctuations. I told them that when I was admitted, and they did x-rays and everything, but they said, "no," as they did n't think that was n't... that was it. Uh, they did n't do a cat scan, they just did an x-ray. I mean, I knew going in that I had n't had a regular bowel movement in a while, even though I drink the miralax every day. I mean, i really do n't think it was a uti. It is, in my humble opinion, that i was just constipated and that was what was causing my blood pressure to be so high. Then when i got home i had a massive poop and i felt much better. But in saying that, you know, inflammation can do that. So, yeah, you could be backed up and inflamed.

Acute to chronic constipation and leukocytosis.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I would recommend just in the next three or four weeks to come back in for some updated fasting labs, and we'll repeat the cbc just to make sure her white blood cells tend downward. Have i ever given you lactulose? It would be a medicine you use in, um, conjunction with miralax. Start lactulose and miralax as appropriate. Schedule follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist. Arrange follow-up in four weeks if symptoms persist. I would like to talk to you about covid, if we can. So where are you at when it comes to the vaccine? So what are your concerns about the vaccine? I do not believe that supplements are necessarily going to help stave this off. I would be concerned if she does n't get the vaccine and I would consider her at high risk based on her quadriplegia status. I think there are side effects from all vaccines, so you have to go back and think about it. So there are definitely potential side effects to the vaccine. I'm not convinced that there's alternative stuff or even the treatment we have, like the monoclonal antibodies, that will give help, but it's just my opinion. So that's what's gon na happen with covid.

section_miscategorizations.text: There were concerns for ongoing urinary tract infection, but she was treated with iv antibiotic therapy. Cultures were obtained and reviewed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This summarizes diagnostic impression and treatment course (concern for UTI, antibiotic therapy) rather than purely objective exam or test results, and belongs in the clinician's synthesis of the problem.

section_miscategorizations.text: So, it looks like you had an infection, right?
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a diagnostic interpretation that the patient had an infection, which is part of the clinician's assessment rather than an objective finding.

section_miscategorizations.text: So I wondered if automatic dysreflexia can affect the white count?
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is diagnostic reasoning about the relationship between dysreflexia and leukocytosis, which fits better in assessment than in the findings section.

section_miscategorizations.text: I told them that when I was admitted, and they did x-rays and everything, but they said, "no," as they did n't think that was n't... that was it. Uh, they did n't do a cat scan, they just did an x-ray.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a patient-reported narrative of prior evaluation and clinician responses in the ED, which belongs in the history of present illness rather than objective current findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: I mean, I knew going in that I had n't had a regular bowel movement in a while, even though I drink the miralax every day.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is patient-reported symptom history and medication use, which should be in HPI, not in the findings section.

section_miscategorizations.text: I mean, i really do n't think it was a uti. It is, in my humble opinion, that i was just constipated and that was what was causing my blood pressure to be so high. Then when i got home i had a massive poop and i felt much better.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is the patient's subjective interpretation of the cause of symptoms and symptom resolution, which belongs in HPI rather than objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: But in saying that, you know, inflammation can do that. So, yeah, you could be backed up and inflamed.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is clinician reasoning about pathophysiology (inflammation from constipation causing symptoms), which is more appropriate for the assessment section than for findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: I would like to talk to you about covid, if we can. So where are you at when it comes to the vaccine? So what are your concerns about the vaccine?
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are conversational prompts to elicit the patient's concerns and history regarding vaccination, which conceptually align more with history gathering than with a concrete treatment plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: I do not believe that supplements are necessarily going to help stave this off. I would be concerned if she does n't get the vaccine and I would consider her at high risk based on her quadriplegia status.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is clinician risk stratification and belief about efficacy of supplements versus vaccination, which is diagnostic/interpretive content and fits better in the assessment than in the plan.

section_miscategorizations.text: I think there are side effects from all vaccines, so you have to go back and think about it. So there are definitely potential side effects to the vaccine.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is general counseling about risk/benefit and side effects, reflecting clinical judgment rather than a specific ordered intervention; it aligns more with assessment/synthesis than with concrete plan items.

section_miscategorizations.text: I'm not convinced that there's alternative stuff or even the treatment we have, like the monoclonal antibodies, that will give help, but it's just my opinion. So that's what's gon na happen with covid.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is clinician opinion about treatment options and disease course, which is interpretive assessment rather than a specific management step.
